results.name: DOCUMENTS GAP: negative milk weight is accepted by the DB
results.detail: ACCEPTED — no CHECK on weight; only the UI stops a negative entry

results.name: DOCUMENTS GAP: fat of 999% is accepted by the DB
results.detail: ACCEPTED — no CHECK on fat range

results.name: DOCUMENTS GAP: payout method accepts any string, not just cash|upi
results.detail: ACCEPTED — method is free text; 'cash'|'upi' is a convention, not a constraint

results.name: DOCUMENTS GAP: ledger kind accepts any string, not just jama|udhar
results.detail: ACCEPTED — kind is free text; ledgerBalance() sums by kind and would silently ignore this row

results.name: a very long name is stored without truncation
results.detail: text column, no length cap — 2000 chars round-tripped

results.name: one bad table in the Promise.all fails the whole pull
updated_at advances on UPDATE (the trigger fires)
members upsert on (society_id, membercode) is idempotent
members upsert returns client_id so pushTable can map rows back
milk_collections upsert on (society_id, client_id) is idempotent
payouts upsert on (society_id, client_id) is idempotent
ledger_entries upsert on (society_id, client_id) is idempotent
local_sales upsert on (society_id, client_id) is idempotent
union_sales upsert on (society_id, client_id) is idempotent
a 500-row batch upsert succeeds in one request (PUSH_CHUNK)
a batch with one bad row rejects the WHOLE batch (all-or-nothing)
results.detail: rejected atomically (23502: null value in column "name" of relation "members" violates not-null constraint)

results.name: pushing a row for a membercode that does not exist still succeeds
results.detail: ACCEPTED — no FK on membercode; orphan collections are possible

results.name: updating a synced collection by remote id works (saveCollectionEdit)
editing another society's collection silently no-ops (saveCollectionEdit reports success)
results.detail: NO ERROR + 0 rows changed → a caller checking only `error` believes it succeeded

results.name: deleting a collection by remote id works (deleteCollection)
backupRateChart creates a Default chart then replaces its entries
defaultChartId uses maybeSingle — two Default charts would break .single()
results.detail: DOCUMENTS GAP: duplicate Default charts are allowed and break restore → PGRST116: JSON object requested, multiple (or no) rows returned

results.name: pullAll reads subscription_end_date + is_active for an active dairy
an expired + inactive dairy reports facts that lock the device
an expired dairy can still PULL (renewal must be able to reach it)
IMPORTANT: an expired dairy can still WRITE to the server (lock is client-only)
results.detail: ACCEPTED — no server-side subscription enforcement; the lock lives only in the app

results.name: active, ends in 30 days → unlocked
active, ends in 1 minute → unlocked
active, expired 1 minute ago → LOCKED
active, expired 5 days ago → LOCKED
INACTIVE but not yet expired → LOCKED
INACTIVE and expired → LOCKED
active, no end date set → LOCKED
INACTIVE, no end date set → LOCKED
EDGE: a fresh install with no subscription data is LOCKED
results.detail: locked before first sync — the reinstall bypass is closed

results.name: EDGE: a malformed subscriptionEnd does NOT lock (NaN comparison)
results.detail: fails OPEN (unlocked) on corrupt data — safer for the farmer, weaker as a gate

results.name: raiseSubscriptionRequest inserts a "requested" payment
SuperAdminScreen.approve extends from the LATER of now / current end
approving a request actually unlocks the dairy
toggling is_active=false locks an otherwise-valid dairy
app_config exposes the payee VPA to signed-in users
the function is reachable
results.detail: NOT DEPLOYED (404) — remaining edge tests are skipped. Nothing in the app calls it, so this is expected.

missing amount → 400
amount = 0 → 400
